integration_map.change: Add reading of BladeLoadCorrFactor from primary FAST input file inside FAST_ReadPrimaryFile
Add BladeLoadCorrFactor to FAST_ParameterType; file is registry-generated, edit AeroDyn_Registry.txt instead if scoping to AD
Inside FAST_InitializeAll, forward BladeLoadCorrFactor from p_FAST into InitInData_AD before calling AD_Init
Declare BladeLoadCorrFactor as ReKi scalar in RotParameterType; regenerate AeroDyn_Types.f90 via OpenFAST registry
Registry-generated; BladeLoadCorrFactor appears in RotParameterType after regeneration; do not edit by hand
Inside SetParameters, assign p%BladeLoadCorrFactor from InputFileData; default to 1.0
Inside RotCalcOutput, pass BladeLoadCorrFactor through to SetOutputsFromBEMT or apply to blade load mesh fields after BEMT_CalcOutput
Inside SetOutputsFromBEMT, multiply y%BladeLoad(k)%Force and Moment arrays by BladeLoadCorrFactor
Optional: if correction applies at blade-element level, add BladeLoadCorrFactor to BEMT_ParameterType and scale Cx/Cy inside BEMT_CalcOutput

pilot_notes: Key tool limitation: analyze_symbol consistently returns CALLEES: none for Fortran subroutines, even for well-defined routines like AD_CalcOutput and BEMT_CalcOutput. Depth-2 callee tracing via the tool alone is not possible; NWTC callee list was reconstructed by cross-referencing module imports (AeroDyn.f90 imports NWTC_Library, BEMT, AirfoilInfo) and running analyze_symbol from the callee side. Tools excel at: (1) directory orientation, (2) locate-by-name with exact line numbers, (3) caller graphs. TYPE declarations are not indexed by analyze_file/analyze_symbol; approx_line estimates for NWTC types are inferred from sibling Copy routine line numbers in registry-generated files. Rubric calibration: AD_CalcOutput:2148 and AD_UpdateStates:1830 are ground-truth checkable; SetOutputsFromBEMT and SetParameters are highest-value integration_map items; MeshMapCreate and Transfer_Line2_to_Point are minimum correct NWTC callee set. Task is tractable but required 59 tool calls; callee graph limitation will affect Haiku more than Sonnet.
